title: Welcome to My Blog
excerpt: Hello! I'm Mark, and this is my personal blog where I'll be sharing my journey through computer science, competitive programming, and various tech adventures.
content:
Welcome to My Blog

Hello! I'm Mark, and this is my personal blog where I'll be sharing my journey through computer science, competitive programming, and various tech adventures.

What to Expect

I'll be writing about:

  • Competitive Programming: Tips, tricks, and problem-solving strategies from my experience in Codeforces and USACO
  • Machine Learning: My explorations in ML and AI
  • Project Updates: Cool things I'm building
  • Tech Thoughts: General musings about technology and programming

About This Site

This website is built with vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. The blog system automatically processes markdown files from the blog directory, making it easy to add new posts.

Get in Touch

Feel free to reach out to me via:

  • Email: xinyunzhouu@gmail.com
  • GitHub: @markjouh
  • Discord: markjouh

Looking forward to sharing more content soon!
markdown:
# Welcome to My Blog

Hello! I'm Mark, and this is my personal blog where I'll be sharing my journey through computer science, competitive programming, and various tech adventures.

## What to Expect

I'll be writing about:

- **Competitive Programming**: Tips, tricks, and problem-solving strategies from my experience in Codeforces and USACO
- **Machine Learning**: My explorations in ML and AI
- **Project Updates**: Cool things I'm building
- **Tech Thoughts**: General musings about technology and programming

## About This Site

This website is built with vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. The blog system automatically processes markdown files from the `blog` directory, making it easy to add new posts.

## Get in Touch

Feel free to reach out to me via:
- Email: xinyunzhouu@gmail.com
- GitHub: [@markjouh](https://github.com/markjouh)
- Discord: markjouh

Looking forward to sharing more content soon!

title: How to Use This Blog System
excerpt: A quick guide on how to add new blog posts using markdown files with front matter
content:
This blog system now automatically scans for markdown files in the blog/ directory. No more editing posts.json!

How to Add a New Post

  1. Create a new .md file in the blog/ directory
  2. Add front matter at the top of your file between --- markers
  3. Write your content in markdown below the front matter

Front Matter Format

---
title: Your Post Title
date: YYYY-MM-DD
excerpt: A brief description of your post (optional)
---

Features

  • Automatic Discovery: Just drop .md files in the blog folder
  • Front Matter Support: Add metadata at the top of your markdown files
  • Date Sorting: Posts are automatically sorted by date (newest first)
  • Excerpt Generation: If you don't provide an excerpt, one will be generated from your content
  • Fallback Support: If no front matter is found, the first heading becomes the title

Example Post

---
title: My Awesome Post
date: 2025-08-01
excerpt: This is a great post about something interesting
---

# Introduction

Your content goes here...

That's it! Just save your markdown file and refresh the blog page.
markdown:
This blog system now automatically scans for markdown files in the `blog/` directory. No more editing `posts.json`!

## How to Add a New Post

1. Create a new `.md` file in the `blog/` directory
2. Add front matter at the top of your file between `---` markers
3. Write your content in markdown below the front matter

## Front Matter Format

```yaml
---
title: Your Post Title
date: YYYY-MM-DD
excerpt: A brief description of your post (optional)
---
```

## Features

- **Automatic Discovery**: Just drop `.md` files in the blog folder
- **Front Matter Support**: Add metadata at the top of your markdown files
- **Date Sorting**: Posts are automatically sorted by date (newest first)
- **Excerpt Generation**: If you don't provide an excerpt, one will be generated from your content
- **Fallback Support**: If no front matter is found, the first heading becomes the title

## Example Post

```markdown
---
title: My Awesome Post
date: 2025-08-01
excerpt: This is a great post about something interesting
---

# Introduction

Your content goes here...
```

That's it! Just save your markdown file and refresh the blog page.
